meta_title: For Students: user onboarding to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: User Onboarding to Solve Project Abandonment
content.seo_description: Learn to implement user onboarding to finally finish your projects. A student-focused guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to boost your portfolio.
content.answer_block: The root cause of unfinished projects is a lack of user momentum. By implementing a structured onboarding flow, you force yourself to define the 'Aha!' moment. This creates a clear roadmap, keeps your development focused, and transforms a messy codebase into a polished, portfolio-ready product that users actually understand.
content.intro:
The graveyard of abandoned GitHub repositories is littered with projects that were 'almost' finished. You start with high-octane energy, drafting complex architectures, but somewhere between the database schema and the third feature, the momentum dies. The code becomes a labyrinth, and you lose sight of why you started in the first place.

But here is the secret: you aren't failing because you lack skill; you are failing because you lack a finish line. By building an onboarding flow first, you are forced to define the exact path a user takes to find value in your app. This constraint acts as a lighthouse, guiding your development and ensuring you build only what matters.

steps.title: Define the 'Aha!' Moment
steps.description: Identify the one action that proves your app provides value. This is the only feature that matters for your MVP onboarding.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my project idea and define the single most important 'Aha!' moment for a new user. Create a 3-step onboarding flow that leads directly to this action.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a user journey map for the core value proposition'

steps.title: Architect the Onboarding State
steps.description: Create a persistent state to track onboarding progress so users don't get stuck if they refresh the page.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a lightweight state management strategy for a 3-step onboarding flow using React Context or local storage.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'implement onboarding state provider with local storage persistence'

steps.title: Build the Interactive Walkthrough
steps.description: Develop a non-intrusive UI component that guides users through your primary feature.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a clean, responsive modal-based onboarding component that highlights the primary call-to-action button.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'create a step-by-step onboarding modal component using Tailwind CSS'

steps.title: Implement Progress Persistence
steps.description: Ensure the user doesn't see the onboarding again once they complete it, preventing frustration.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a hook that checks for onboarding completion status and redirects the user to the dashboard if already finished.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'add useOnboarding hook to manage user completion status'

steps.title: Automate Onboarding Analytics
steps.description: Track where users drop off in your onboarding to identify UI friction points early.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add basic event tracking to the onboarding steps to log when a user completes each phase.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'add event tracking for each onboarding step completion'

pro_tips: Never build onboarding for more than 3 steps; if it takes longer, your app is too complex for an MVP.
Use a 'Skip' button. Forcing users to learn your UI is the fastest way to lose them.
Build the onboarding flow using dummy data first so you can test the UI without needing a full backend.
Highlight only one UI element per step to prevent cognitive overload.
Treat your onboarding as a product feature, not an afterthought; document it in your portfolio as a UX design choice.
content.next_steps: Once your onboarding is functional, deploy your MVP to Vercel or Netlify and share it with three friends to get real-world feedback on your flow.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to stop building aimlessly and start shipping finished products. Stop doubting your potential and start treating your code like a professional asset. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
